text.title: MLP Planning Workflow
text.summary: Five-mode sequential MLP pipeline that transforms a shipped MVP into a Most Lovable Product, with constraints on WOW feature grounding and a cap of 7 WOW items.

section.title: MLP Five-Mode Sequence

rule.statement: Run the five faion-mlp-agent modes strictly in sequence: analyze → find-gaps → propose → update → plan. Each mode persists its artifact so a failed run can resume from the last completed mode.
rule.rationale: Skipping modes — especially running propose before analyze — produces WOW features ungrounded in competitor evidence, which is the primary MLP failure mode.

section.p: Output artifacts: product_docs/mvp-scope-analysis.md (analyze), product_docs/mlp-analysis-report.md (find-gaps), product_docs/mlp-implementation-order.md (plan). Each file is the input to the next mode.

rule.statement: Every WOW feature must cite a row in mvp-scope-analysis.md; features with no competitor evidence are labeled "speculative" and placed under "Deferred — needs validation".
rule.rationale: Speculative WOW features not grounded in competitor evidence or customer data are guesses; they belong in the deferred bucket, not the MLP roadmap.

rule.statement: Cap WOW features at 7; more than 7 and the MLP becomes a v2.0 plan, not an MLP.
rule.rationale: MLP is the minimum set of WOW features that makes users love the product — a superset of MVP but not a full feature rebuild. Empirically, 8+ WOW features require a second release cycle to ship.

rule.statement: Run MLP propose only after the MVP ships and produces real telemetry; propose-without-data is the primary failure mode the workflow does not warn about in its README.
rule.rationale: WOW feature selection without usage data is marketing speculation; the propose mode needs to know which MVP features users actually used before proposing enhancements.

antipattern.description: MLP "update" mode rewrites spec files in-place on retry, clobbering FR numbering.
antipattern.reason: Always run git status before and after update mode; agents on retry have been observed overwriting FR-NN.N numbering with duplicates. Use the artifact files as checkpoints, not as editable scratch space.

antipattern.description: Running the full MLP pipeline from cron when the product is still in MVP phase with no telemetry.
antipattern.reason: MLP propose generates fictional WOW features when there is no usage data; the cron trigger must check for the existence of a telemetry artifact before running.
